units.title: Introduction to Instrumentation System
sub_topics: Typical applications of Instrument systems

Functional elements of Instrumentation and Measuring systems i.e., Input elements (Transducers and Electrodes), intermediate elements (signal conditioning) and output elements (Data display and storage)

Errors and uncertainties in Measurements and Static performance characteristics of instruments: Introduction to errors and uncertainties; Static performance parameters: Accuracy, Precision, Resolution, Threshold, Sensitivity, Linearity, Hysteresis, Dead band, Backlash, Drift, Span; Impedance loading and matching; Statistical analysis of error in measurement; Standards of measurement
Bridge Measurement: DC bridges- Wheatstone bridge; AC bridges - Kelvin, Hay, Maxwell, Schering and Wien bridges; Wagner ground Connection

Physical Variable and Transducer: Physical Variable and their types (Electrical, Mechanical, Process and Biophysical); Transducer principle and operation; Input and output characteristics and application of transducers: Resistive, Capacitive, Inductive
Measurement of mechanical variables: displacement, strain, velocity, acceleration and vibration
Measurement of process variables: temperature, pressure, level, fluid flow, chemical constituents in gases or liquids, pH and humidity
Measurement of bio-physical variables: blood pressure and myoelectric potentials
Calibration and error in transducers
Measurement of voltage & current (moving coil & moving iron instruments)
Measurement of low, high & medium resistances

Types of DAC: Weighted Resistor DAC, R-2R Ladder DAC, PWM Type DAC

sub_topics: Explosives: Introduction, preparation, properties and applications of TNT and TNG

sub_topics: Laser: Introduction, Principles of generation (induced absorption, spontaneous emission, stimulated emission, population inversion, pumping, metastable state), He-Ne laser, Semiconductor laser, Applications

units.title: Capacitor and Dielectric
sub_topics: Capacitor: Introduction, Types, Charging and discharging

units.title: Electromagnetism
sub_topics: EM Oscillation: LC oscillation, Damped LCR oscillation, Forced electromagnetic oscillation, resonance and quality factor

EM waves: Maxwell equations in integral form, Conversion to differential form, Continuity equation, Relation between electric field, magnetic field and speed of light, Wave equations in free space, verification of light as EM wave, Wave equation in dielectric medium

sub_topics: Inadequacy of classical mechanics, Importance of quantum mechanics
Matter wave (de-Broglie equation)
Wave function and its significance, Energy and momentum operator
Time independent and time dependent Schrodinger wave equations

Normalized wave function describing the motion of an electron inside an infinite potential well

units.title: Fundamentals of Thermodynamics and Heat Transfer
sub_topics: Concepts and definition: applications

Laws of thermodynamics: first law for closed system, internal and stored energy, joules law, enthalpy, specific heat, applications, second law, heat engine, refrigerator and heat pump, COP, Kelvin-Planck and Clausius statements

Heat transfer: modes (conduction, convection and radiation), Fourier's law, one dimensional steady state heat conduction through plane wall, laws of radiation (Emissive power, emissivity, Stefan-Boltzmann law), concept of black bodies

units.title: Matrix and System of linear equations
sub_topics: System of linear equations, classification, matrix and vector representations, solution by Cramer's and Gauss elimination methods

sub_topics: Vectors and vector spaces, R² and R³ as vector spaces and their subspaces, Basis

Eigenvalues and eigenvectors, characteristic equation, computation, properties
Cayley-Hamilton theorem (statement and verification only), application to compute inverse of a matrix (up to 3×3)
Similar matrices and diagonalization of matrix (up to 2×2 matrices only)

sub_topics: Introduction, Model Formulation, Standard Form, Solution by Simplex Method

sub_topics: Infinite sequence and concept of convergence, infinite series, invariance of convergence, necessary condition, convergence of infinite geometric series

Alternating series: Leibnitz test, absolute convergence, power series, interval and radius of convergence

sub_topics: Transformation of coordinates: by translation, by rotation, combined transformation, use to reduce complex equations

General equation of Conic section in Cartesian and Polar form

units.title: Three-dimensional Geometry
sub_topics: Review coordinate in space and plane

Straight line: symmetrical form, line through two points, reduction of general equation, angle between plane and line, conditions for a line to lie on a plane, length of perpendicular, coplanar lines, condition for intersection, shortest distance
Sphere: Equation, condition for general equation to represent a sphere, diameter form, plane section, circle as intersection, intersection of two spheres, tangent plane, condition of tangency
Cone: Equation with given vertex and generator intersecting given conic, right circular cone
Cylinder: Equation whose generator intersects a given conic and is parallel to a given line, right circular cylinder

sub_topics: Philosophy of Data Structures: Need, Characteristics and Types

Algorithm Design Techniques: Divide and Conquer, Greedy Algorithms, Backtracking
Algorithm Analysis: Best, Worst and Average Case Analysis, Rate of Growth, Asymptotic Notations (Big Oh, Big Omega and Big Theta)

sub_topics: Stack: Definition, Operations, Stack ADT and Array Implementation, Expression Evaluation (Infix and Postfix), Expression Conversion (Infix to Postfix and Postfix to Infix)

Recursion: Problem Solving Technique, Principle of Recursion, Recursive Algorithms (GCD, Sum of Natural Numbers, Factorial, Fibonacci Series, Tower of Hanoi), Recursion and Stack, Recursion vs Iteration, Recursive Data Structures, Types and Applications of Recursion
